case_title: YAKOV KUSHNIRSKY VS. LEN TOLSTUNOV et al

SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT, PROOF OF SERVICE ONLY, FILED BY PLAINTIFF KUSHNIRSKY, YAKOV SERVED APR-29-2009, PERSONAL SERVICE AS TO DEFENDANT TOLSTUNOV, LEN

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT VAN NESS ORAL SURGERY & IMPLANTOLOGY CENTER, LEN TOLSTUNOV, D.D.S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S CAUSE OF ACTION GENERL NEGLIGENCE FROM PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO STRIKE GRANTED WITH FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MAY NOT MAINTAIN SEPARATE AND INDEPENDENT CLAIMS FOR GENERAL AND PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE BASED UPON THE SAME CONDUCT. SEE FLOWERS VS. TORRANCE MEMORIAL HOSPITAL MEDICAL CENTER (1994) 8 CAL. 4TH, 992. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-24-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT VAN NESS ORAL SURGERY & IMPLANTOLOGY CENTER, LEN TOLSTUNOV, D.D.S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S CAUSE OF ACTION GENERL NEGLIGENCE FROM PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. Appearances: Scott D. Peebles, of Peeples Law Offices (415) 287-4090, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Michael Kowalski, Esq. of Bradley Curley Asiano Barrabee & Gale PC (415) 464-8888, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike granted with fifteen days leave to amend. Plaintiff may not maintain separate and independent claims for general and professional negligence based upon the same conduct. See Flowers vs. Torrance Memorial Hospital Medical Center (1994) 8 Cal. 4th, 992. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
